data.title: Clorox to acquire GOJO Industries for $2.25B in cash
data.description:
Bleach maker Clorox (CLX) on Thursday said it would acquire manufacturer of hand hygiene and skin care products GOJO Industries for $2.25 billion in cash.

The transaction includes anticipated tax benefits valued at about $330 million for a purchase price of $1.92 billion, Clorox said.

GOJO, which was founded in 1946 by Goldie and Jerry Lippman, is known for its Purell hand sanitizer.

“This is a compelling acquisition that evolves our portfolio and scales our fastest growing, most profitable operating segment – Health and Wellness - as we execute our IGNITE strategy to deliver long-term shareholder value,” said Clorox CEO Linda Rendle.

The company said the deal supports its long-term sales growth target of 3-5% and is accretive to Clorox's adjusted EPS in the second year.

Clorox added that the deal value represents an adjusted EBITDA multiple of 11.9x net of anticipated tax benefits and an adjusted EBITDA multiple of 9.1x net of anticipated tax benefits and run-rate cost synergies.

Clorox said it plans to fund the transaction primarily through debt financing. The transaction is expected to be completed before the end of Clorox's fiscal year 2026.

Excluding the impact from the transaction, Clorox also reaffirmed its fiscal year 2026 outlook for net sales, diluted EPS and adjusted EPS as provided in its Nov. 3, 2025 earnings release when the company guided to the lower end of the range.

Shares of Clorox were trading 0.7% lower in extended trading on Thursday.

data.title: C3is to buy two MR product tankers
data.description:
• C3is (CISS) on Thursday said it has agreed to acquire two medium range product tankers for a total of $39.8 million, expanding its fleet by 50%.
  • The company said it signed memoranda of agreement to buy a 2008-built tanker for $16.88 million and a 2011-built tanker for $22.90 million.
  • The vessels, each with about 50,000 deadweight tons capacity, are expected to be delivered between the first and third quarters of 2026.
  • Following the acquisitions, C3is’ fleet will increase to six vessels, comprising three Handysize dry bulk carriers and three tankers, including an Aframax oil tanker and the two MR product tankers.
  • The vessels will be acquired from an entity affiliated with Brave Maritime Corp C3is said the deals were approved by its independent directors and will be funded with a one-year interest-free loan.
  • CISS closed -34.23% at $0.0928.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Capital One to buy fintech Brex for $5.15B
data.description:
Capital One Financial (COF) agreed to acquire Brex, a fintech that specializes in technology for credit cards, expenses, and rewards, for $5.15B, the company said on Thursday.

The purchase price consists of 50% cash and 50% stock. With the acquisition, Capital One (COF), which acquired Discover Financial last year, gets more capabilities to cater to corporate clients.

The Wall Street Journal first reported the deal earlier on Thursday. Brex was founded in 2017 to serve other startups that were finding it challenging to get corporate cards from traditional companies like American Express (AXP), the report said.

Capital One (COF) expects to close the deal in Q2, the people told the WSJ.

data.title: CSX reports Q4 results
data.description:
• CSX press release (CSX): Q4 EPS was $0.39, compared to adjusted EPS of $0.42 in the prior year.
  • Revenue of $3.51B (-0.8% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Operating income was $1.11 billion, compared to adjusted operating income of $1.21 billion in the prior year. Operating margin was 31.6%, compared to operating margin of 31.3% and adjusted operating margin of 34.3% in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Fourth quarter operating income and EPS include $50 million and $0.02, respectively, in severance and technology rationalization expense.
